message:
refactor(robot): use bv.BeadsSummary type from bv package

Consolidates BeadsSummary type definition in the bv package and updates
robot.go to use it, eliminating duplication.

Changes:
- Replace local BeadsSummary with bv.BeadsSummary
- Replace local BeadPreview with bv.BeadPreview
- Replace local BeadInProgress with bv.BeadInProgress
- Call bv.GetBeadsSummary() instead of local getBeadsSummary()
- Remove duplicate type definitions from robot.go

This improves type consistency and enables other packages to use
the same beads summary types without depending on robot package.

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

 {"id":"ntm-nts","title":"Add Agent Mail to robot mode output","description":"# Add Agent Mail to Robot Mode Output

## Purpose

Extend --robot-status and add --robot-mail for AI orchestrators to access Agent Mail state programmatically.

## Enhanced --robot-status

Add agent_mail section to existing robot status:
```json
{
  "sessions": [...],
  "summary": {...},
  "agent_mail": {
    "available": true,
    "server_url": "http://127.0.0.1:8765/mcp/",
    "sessions_registered": 3,
    "total_unread": 7,
    "total_locks": 5,
    "urgent_messages": 2
  }
}
```

## New --robot-mail Flag

```bash
ntm --robot-mail [session]
```

Output:
```json
{
  "session": "myproject",
  "agent_mail": {
    "connected": true,
    "session_agent": {
      "name": "ntm_myproject",
      "project_key": "/path/to/myproject",
      "registered_at": "2025-12-10T10:00:00Z",
      "last_active": "2025-12-10T15:30:00Z"
    },
    "agents": [
      {
        "pane": "cc_1",
        "agent_name": "GreenCastle",
        "program": "claude-code",
        "model": "opus-4.1",
        "unread_count": 2,
        "urgent_count": 1
      }
    ],
    "messages": {
      "total": 15,
      "unread": 3,
      "urgent": 1,
      "pending_ack": 2
    },
    "file_reservations": [
      {
        "id": 42,
        "pattern": "src/api/**",
        "agent": "GreenCastle",
        "exclusive": true,
        "expires_in_seconds": 2700,
        "reason": "Implementing endpoints"
      }
    ],
    "conflicts": []
  }
}
```

## Use Cases for AI Orchestrators

1. **Check before sending work**: Are there conflicts? Who has locks?
2. **Monitor coordination**: Are agents communicating? Any pending acks?
3. **Detect stale locks**: Reservations expiring soon
4. **Route messages**: Know which agent to send to based on locks

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] --robot-status includes agent_mail summary
- [ ] --robot-mail provides detailed per-session state
- [ ] Includes all registered agents with pane mapping
- [ ] Message counts by status (unread, urgent, pending_ack)
- [ ] File reservations with expiry info
- [ ] Conflict detection included","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-10T03:19:45.615031-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-10T03:19:45.615031-05:00","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"ntm-nts","depends_on_id":"ntm-161","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-10T03:19:56.365366-05:00","created_by":"daemon"},{"issue_id":"ntm-nts","depends_on_id":"ntm-1ts","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-10T03:19:56.463319-05:00","created_by":"daemon"}]}
 {"id":"ntm-nuf","title":"E2E Test: Full Agent Workflow","description":"# E2E Test: Full Agent Workflow

## Scenario
Complete lifecycle of spawning, using, and killing an agent session.

## Steps
1. ntm spawn testproject --cc=2 --cod=1
2. Verify session exists with 4 panes
3. ntm send testproject --cc 'echo hello'
4. Verify 'echo hello' appears in Claude panes
5. ntm send testproject --cod 'echo world'
6. Verify 'echo world' appears in Codex pane
7. ntm interrupt testproject
8. Verify Ctrl+C sent to all panes
9. ntm status testproject --json
10. Verify JSON is valid and accurate
11. ntm kill -f testproject
12. Verify session no longer exists

## Logging Requirements
```
[2025-12-10T10:30:45Z] TEST START: TestFullAgentWorkflow
[2025-12-10T10:30:45Z] SETUP: Environment check
[2025-12-10T10:30:45Z]   tmux version: 3.3a
[2025-12-10T10:30:45Z]   ntm version: dev
[2025-12-10T10:30:45Z]   existing sessions: 0
[2025-12-10T10:30:45Z] STEP 1: Spawn session
[2025-12-10T10:30:45Z]   EXEC: ntm spawn ntm_e2e_12345 --cc=2 --cod=1
[2025-12-10T10:30:46Z]   STDOUT: Session 'ntm_e2e_12345' created (4 panes)
[2025-12-10T10:30:46Z]   EXIT: 0
[2025-12-10T10:30:46Z] STEP 2: Verify session
[2025-12-10T10:30:46Z]   VERIFY: tmux has-session -t ntm_e2e_12345
[2025-12-10T10:30:46Z]   RESULT: exists
[2025-12-10T10:30:46Z]   VERIFY: pane count == 4
[2025-12-10T10:30:46Z]   RESULT: PASS
...
[2025-12-10T10:30:50Z] TEARDOWN: Killing test session
[2025-12-10T10:30:50Z] TEST END: PASS (5.2s)
```

## Implementation
- tests/e2e/agent_workflow_test.go
- Custom test harness with logging
- Unique session names per run
- Full cleanup even on failure","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-10T03:23:54.575845-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-10T03:23:54.575845-05:00","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"ntm-nuf","depends_on_id":"ntm-66c","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-10T03:24:41.867795-05:00","created_by":"daemon"},{"issue_id":"ntm-nuf","depends_on_id":"ntm-vr2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-10T03:24:42.979512-05:00","created_by":"daemon"}]}
 {"id":"ntm-nxf","title":"Integrate context status into dashboard","description":"# Integrate Context Status into Dashboard

## Purpose

Show context usage in the dashboard view for at-a-glance monitoring.

## Dashboard Enhancement

Add context bar to each pane card:
```
┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
│ Panes (4)                                                   │
├─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┤
│ ● cc_1   opus      working    [████████░░] 62%              │
│ ● cc_2   sonnet    idle       [█████████░] 89% ⚠️           │
│ ● cod_1  gpt4      working    [███░░░░░░░] 31%              │
│ ● gmi_1  pro       idle       [░░░░░░░░░░]  5%              │
└─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘
```

## Implementation

Add context field to pane model in dashboard:
```go
type PaneCard struct {
    // Existing fields...
    ContextUsed int
    ContextMax  int
    ContextPct  float64
}
```

Render context bar:
```go
func (p PaneCard) ContextBar(width int) string {
    filled := int(p.ContextPct * float64(width) / 100)
    empty := width - filled
    
    color := theme.Green
    if p.ContextPct > 80 {
        color = theme.Red
    } else if p.ContextPct > 60 {
        color = theme.Yellow
    }
    
    return color.Render(strings.Repeat("█", filled)) + 
           theme.Dim.Render(strings.Repeat("░", empty))
}
```

## Refresh Strategy

- Context data cached for 60s
- Press 'c' to force context refresh
- Background refresh if dashboard open >60s

## Keyboard Shortcut

| Key | Action |
|-----|--------|
| c | Refresh context data |
| C | Open full context view |

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] Context bar displayed per pane
- [ ] Color coding (green/yellow/red)
- [ ] Warning icon for >80%
- [ ] Keyboard shortcut for refresh
- [ ] Graceful handling when data unavailable","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-10T03:26:13.013354-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-10T03:26:13.013354-05:00","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"ntm-nxf","depends_on_id":"ntm-jda","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-10T03:26:23.526527-05:00","created_by":"daemon"}]}
-{"id":"ntm-oba","title":"Implement --robot-terse for ultra-compact output","description":"Single-line encoded state for extremely token-constrained scenarios.

## Rationale
Sometimes I need just the essentials in minimum tokens. A full snapshot might be 500+ tokens; terse mode gives me the critical state in <50 tokens.

## Usage
```bash
ntm --robot-terse
ntm --robot-snapshot --terse  # terse snapshot
```

## Output Format
```
S:myproject|A:3/3|R:30|B:27|I:3|M:5|!:2
```

Decoded:
- S: Session name
- A: Agents (active/total)
- R: Ready beads
- B: Blocked beads  
- I: In-progress beads
- M: Unread mail
- !: Alert count

## Multi-Session
```
S:proj1|A:2/2|R:15|B:10|I:1|M:2|!:0;S:proj2|A:3/3|R:20|B:5|I:2|M:0|!:1
```

## Use Cases
1. Polling in tight loops
2. Embedding in prompts with strict token limits
3. Quick health checks
4. Dashboard status bars

## Parsing
Provide parsing utilities in robot package:
```go
func ParseTerse(s string) *TerseState
```","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-10T02:52:39.009743-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-10T02:52:39.009743-05:00","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"ntm-oba","depends_on_id":"ntm-sim","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-10T02:54:39.818091-05:00","created_by":"daemon"}]}

 {"id":"ntm-oln","title":"Implement --robot-spawn for structured session creation","description":"Create session with preset and return structured result.

## Rationale
Current spawn returns human-readable output. For orchestration, I need structured confirmation of exactly what was created.

## Usage
```bash
ntm --robot-spawn myproject --preset=fullstack
ntm --robot-spawn myproject --cc=2 --cod=1
```

## Output Structure
```json
{
  "session": "myproject",
  "created_at": "ISO8601",
  "preset_used": "fullstack",
  "working_dir": "/path/to/project",
  "agents": [
    {"pane": "0.0", "type": "claude", "ready": true, "startup_ms": 234},
    {"pane": "0.1", "type": "claude", "ready": true, "startup_ms": 198},
    {"pane": "0.2", "type": "codex", "ready": true, "startup_ms": 312}
  ],
  "layout": "tiled",
  "total_startup_ms": 512
}
```

## Ready Detection
- Parse pane output for agent prompt/ready indicator
- Timeout after configurable wait (default 30s)
- Report ready=false with reason if agent didn't start

## Integration
Works with recipe system when implemented. Preset name maps to recipe definition.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"PinkCreek","created_at":"2025-12-10T02:52:55.090595-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-10T09:05:35.50312-05:00","closed_at":"2025-12-10T09:05:35.50312-05:00"}
